description: Vertical, portrait-orientation 'Labor News' headline card from the U.S. Department of Labor. The upper portion is a black-and-white, blue-grey toned photograph of a classroom: an older grey-haired man in a collared shirt and eyeglasses, seen from behind and the side at right, gestures toward a group of seated adult students who face him; several students (a woman in front with dark hair, a blonde woman, others) are blurred in the background. The lower portion fades to a dark navy gradient. A faint embossed Department of Labor seal watermark sits at the lower right. A small blue rectangular tab with white italic serif text reads 'Labor News', next to three short vertical bars. Below it, large white serif headline text reads: 'US Department of Labor announces availability of $65M in grants to help community colleges increase access to in-demand, high-quality training.'
notable_text: Labor News - US Department of Labor announces availability of $65M in grants to help community colleges increase access to in-demand, high-quality training
